summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function: a nightly Timer-triggered Python 3.11 function that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI, validates the roster response, and persists structured JSON to a dedicated Azure Blob Storage account using Managed Identity. Covers Bicep IaC, five GitHub Actions workflows, TRAPI client with retry logic, schema validation, conditional blob writes, structured observability, and full release pipeline through staging slot swap to production.
assumptions: TRAPI endpoint https://trapi.research.microsoft.com/gcr/shared is publicly accessible from Azure Consumption Plan egress IPs with no VNet injection required.
Managed Identity bearer token with scope api://trapi/.default is sufficient for TRAPI authentication; no additional API key is needed.
The specific GPT-4o model version to pin will be confirmed by rciapala before APP-01 is implemented.
Azure subscription and resource group already exist or will be created by the CD Setup workflow.

Deployment slots (staging, production) are available; Consumption Plan supports slot swap via Azure Functions Premium or at minimum a blue/green resource group swap if slots are unavailable on Consumption.
Existing repo files (trapi_client.py, blob_writer.py) represent prior work that will be refactored/replaced to meet spec requirements.
pytest and pytest-cov are the approved test runners per existing requirements.txt.
All new application source files are placed under /src per repo coding standards.
Bicep modules under /infra will be extended (not replaced) where prior work exists.
Email notification target for alerts is rciapala (email address to be confirmed for Action Group config).
open_questions: What is the exact GPT-4o model version string to pin in the TRAPI prompt request (e.g., gpt-4o-2024-05-13)?
Does TRAPI support Managed Identity bearer token auth at scope api://trapi/.default, or is a client credential stored in Key Vault required?
Is deployment slot swap available on the Consumption Plan, or does CD Promote need to implement a blue/green resource-group-level promotion strategy?
What is the rciapala email address for the Application Insights Action Group?
Are there IP allowlist or conditional access policies on TRAPI that require static outbound IPs (which would necessitate a Premium plan or NAT gateway)?
Should the host storage account and the dedicated data storage account be in the same resource group?
Is there an existing Azure AD tenant app registration for TRAPI, or does one need to be requested?
Should the /api/health HTTP trigger be implemented, or is the Timer Trigger monitor sufficient for ops health checks?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Provision all Azure infrastructure, configure identity and RBAC, and establish the CD Setup pipeline so subsequent app and hardening work has a stable, deployable target environment.
entry_criteria: Azure subscription and resource group available
Repo access and branch permissions confirmed
TRAPI auth scope confirmed (or assumption documented)
exit_criteria: All Bicep modules lint and deploy cleanly to a dev/test resource group
System-assigned Managed Identity exists on Function App with Storage Blob Data Contributor and Key Vault Secrets User role assignments verified
CD Setup workflow executes successfully and OIDC federated credential is active
TRAPI auth spike result documented: Managed Identity token exchange confirmed or Key Vault secret path identified

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement all application logic: TRAPI client, response validator, blob writer, timer trigger function, failed-response handler, structured logging, and the pinned prompt template.
exit_criteria: GetAndStoreYankeesRoster function deploys and runs locally against TRAPI
Structured log events emitted for all key stages
player_count_returned custom metric emitted on successful run

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability controls, security hardening, full CI/CD pipelines, Application Insights alerting, and complete unit and integration test coverage.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function runs end-to-end in dev environment
exit_criteria: All unit tests pass with coverage on trapi_client, validator, and blob_writer
Integration test asserts Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson appear in blob output
All three Application Insights alert rules active in staging
CI, CD-Infra, CD-App, and CD-Promote workflows execute without errors
Key Vault references resolve correctly in staging Function App settings

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in staging, promote to production via slot swap, confirm monitoring is live, and publish complete documentation.
entry_criteria: Staging environment fully provisioned and function deployed
exit_criteria: Nightly timer verified to fire at 02:00 UTC in staging with valid blob output
Production deployment completed via CD Promote slot swap
All three alert rules active in production
README complete with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, blob naming, and CI/CD instructions
All success criteria in spec.md checked off

tasks.title: Validate TRAPI auth mechanism and pin GPT-4o model version
tasks.description: Run a manual spike to confirm that the Function App system-assigned Managed Identity can acquire a bearer token for scope api://trapi/.default and successfully call the TRAPI endpoint. Pin the exact GPT-4o model version string. If Managed Identity auth fails, document the Key Vault secret path required. Update spec.md TRAPI endpoint and auth fields with confirmed values.
inputs: TRAPI team contact for auth scope confirmation
outputs: docs/trapi-auth-spike.md — confirmed auth mechanism, model version string, and any required Key Vault secret path
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI endpoint reachable from Azure egress (curl test from Function App Kudu console or equivalent)
Managed Identity token acquired for scope api://trapi/.default OR Key Vault secret path documented
GPT-4o model version string confirmed and recorded (e.g., gpt-4o-2024-05-13)
spec.md updated with confirmed TRAPI endpoint and auth scope values
Spike result reviewed and signed off by rciapala
risks: TRAPI may require additional Azure AD app registration or allow-listing of the Managed Identity principal — blocks APP-01
Model version pinning may be unavailable via TRAPI gateway, requiring prompt-level workaround

tasks.title: Bicep: provision dedicated data Storage Account
tasks.description: Author or update infra/modules/storage.bicep to provision the dedicated Standard_LRS storage account with Cool access tier, allowSharedKeyAccess:false, soft-delete enabled (7-day retention), and the private yankees-roster container (publicAccess:None). Ensure the host Function App storage account is a separate resource.
outputs: infra/modules/storage.bicep — updated with Cool tier, soft-delete, dedicated account params
acceptance_criteria: Storage account kind StorageV2, sku Standard_LRS, accessTier Cool
allowSharedKeyAccess: false
blobServiceProperties softDelete enabled with retentionDays: 7
Container yankees-roster created with publicAccess: None
Host storage account (AzureWebJobsStorage) is a separate resource from the data storage account
Bicep lints cleanly (az bicep build)
risks: Cool access tier has early deletion penalty for blobs deleted before 30 days — acceptable given 7-day soft-delete retention policy

tasks.title: Bicep: provision Function App with system-assigned Managed Identity
tasks.description: Author or update infra/modules/functionapp.bicep to provision a Linux Consumption Plan Function App (Python 3.11) with system-assigned Managed Identity, APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting, AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName identity-based binding, and functionTimeout/maxConcurrentCalls values from host.json. Include a staging deployment slot.
outputs: infra/modules/functionapp.bicep — updated with staging slot, identity, app settings
acceptance_criteria: Function App kind: functionapp,linux; reserved: true; linuxFxVersion: Python|3.11
System-assigned Managed Identity enabled
Staging deployment slot provisioned
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName set (no listKeys / no connection string)
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING set via Key Vault reference or output from INF-04
Bicep lints cleanly
risks: Consumption Plan may not support slot swap natively — confirm slot support or plan alternative blue/green strategy for CD-Promote

tasks.title: Bicep: provision Azure Key Vault
tasks.description: Author infra/modules/keyvault.bicep to provision a zone-redundant Key Vault with RBAC authorization mode (not access policies), soft-delete enabled, and purge protection enabled. Wire Key Vault URI into the Function App app settings for Key Vault references.
inputs: spec.md — secrets and Key Vault section
outputs: infra/modules/keyvault.bicep — new module
infra/main.bicep — updated to include keyvault module
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault created with enableRbacAuthorization: true
softDeleteRetentionInDays: 90, enablePurgeProtection: true
Zone-redundant (sku: premium or standard with zone redundancy param)
Key Vault URI output passed to Function App module for @Microsoft.KeyVault() references
Bicep lints cleanly
risks: Zone-redundant Key Vault requires Premium SKU in some regions — validate region support

tasks.title: Bicep: provision Log Analytics Workspace and Application Insights
tasks.description: Author or update infra/modules/monitoring.bicep to provision a Log Analytics Workspace (30-day retention) and a workspace-based Application Insights instance with sampling enabled. Output APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING for use in Function App app settings.
outputs: infra/modules/monitoring.bicep — workspace-based App Insights with 30-day retention, sampling config
acceptance_criteria: Log Analytics Workspace retentionInDays: 30
Application Insights type: web, IngestionMode: LogAnalytics, workspace-based
Adaptive sampling enabled (or fixed-rate sampling configured in host.json)
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING output available for Function App wiring
Bicep lints cleanly
risks: Workspace-based App Insights required for alert queries — classic App Insights will not support the scheduled query alert rules in OBS-01

tasks.title: Bicep: configure all RBAC role assignments for Managed Identity
tasks.description: Author or update infra/modules/rbac.bicep to assign Storage Blob Data Contributor (scoped to yankees-roster container), Storage Queue Data Contributor (host storage), and Key Vault Secrets User (Key Vault) to the Function App system-assigned Managed Identity. All assignments must be scoped as narrowly as possible.
outputs: infra/modules/rbac.bicep — all three role assignments with minimal scope
acceptance_criteria: Storage Blob Data Contributor assigned at container scope (not account scope)
Key Vault Secrets User assigned at Key Vault scope
Storage Queue Data Contributor assigned for host storage AzureWebJobsStorage identity-based binding
No Owner or Contributor role assignments granted to the Function App identity
Bicep lints cleanly; role assignment GUIDs are deterministic (using guid() function)
risks: Container-scoped RBAC for blob requires the container to exist before the role assignment — ensure storage module creates container before rbac module runs

tasks.title: GitHub Actions: CD Setup workflow (OIDC federated identity)
tasks.description: Implement .github/workflows/cd-setup.yml that provisions an Azure AD app registration with federated identity credential for GitHub Actions OIDC. The workflow runs manually, creates the service principal, assigns it Contributor on the resource group, and outputs the AZURE_CLIENT_ID, AZURE_TENANT_ID, AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID values for the operator to store as GitHub repository variables (not secrets for client ID/tenant/subscription — only OIDC, no client secrets).
outputs: .github/workflows/cd-setup.yml — manual trigger OIDC setup workflow
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers manually (workflow_dispatch)
Uses azure/login action with OIDC (no client_secret)
Creates federated identity credential scoped to the correct repo and branch/environment
No client secrets or credentials stored in workflow YAML or repository secrets
Workflow output documents which GitHub variables/secrets must be set by the operator
README.md updated with step-by-step OIDC setup instructions
risks: Operator running CD Setup must have Azure AD application administrator rights — may require elevated access request

tasks.title: Create pinned prompt template for TRAPI GPT-4o query
tasks.description: Create prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt with the exact GPT-4o prompt instructing the model to return the 1985 New York Yankees active roster in the specified JSON schema. Embed the pinned model version as a comment or companion config file. The prompt must be deterministic and version-controlled.
inputs: spec.md — Requirement 1 prompt template and response schema
outputs: prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt — prompt template
prompts/model_config.json — pinned model version and TRAPI request params
acceptance_criteria: prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt committed to source control
Prompt instructs model to return JSON matching schema: {players: [{name, position, jersey_number}]}
Prompt explicitly requests active roster only (24–28 players)
prompts/model_config.json specifies pinned model version string confirmed in DES-01
Prompt hash can be computed at runtime for inclusion in trapi_request_sent log event
risks: GPT-4o output may vary slightly even with temperature=0 — prompt should explicitly constrain output format to reduce schema validation failures

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with Managed Identity auth and retry logic
tasks.description: Implement src/trapi_client.py to authenticate via DefaultAzureCredential with scope api://trapi/.default, load the pinned prompt from prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt, POST to the TRAPI endpoint, and return the parsed JSON response. Enforce a 45-second HTTP timeout and 3 exponential-backoff retries on transient failures (5xx, timeout). Emit trapi_request_sent and trapi_response_received structured log events.
outputs: src/trapi_client.py — TRAPI client with auth, retry, timeout, structured logging
acceptance_criteria: DefaultAzureCredential used; no hardcoded credentials
HTTP timeout of 45 seconds enforced on TRAPI POST
3 retries with exponential backoff (e.g., 2s, 4s, 8s) on 5xx and timeout errors
4xx errors are not retried (fail fast)
trapi_request_sent log event includes model_version and prompt_hash fields
trapi_response_received log event includes token_count, latency_ms, and raw player_count fields
Module-level DefaultAzureCredential instance reused across calls (not re-instantiated per call)
risks: TRAPI rate limits or quota constraints could cause retries to exhaust — add jitter to backoff
Token acquisition latency on cold start adds to overall function duration

tasks.title: Implement response schema validator and player count check
tasks.description: Implement src/validator.py to validate the TRAPI response against the expected JSON schema ({players: [{name, position, jersey_number}]}) and assert the player count is within [24, 28]. Return a typed result indicating success or failure with a descriptive error message for logging and alerting.
inputs: spec.md — Requirement 1 acceptance criteria and response schema
outputs: src/validator.py — schema and count validator with typed ValidationResult
acceptance_criteria: Validates players array exists and is non-empty
Each player object has name (str), position (str), jersey_number (int) fields
Rejects responses with player count < 24 or > 28
Returns a structured ValidationResult(success: bool, player_count: int, error: str|None)
Known player assertion function available for integration tests (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson)
risks: GPT-4o may return jersey_number as string — validator should coerce or reject with clear error

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and Managed Identity
tasks.description: Implement src/blob_writer.py to write validated roster JSON to the dedicated storage account using BlobServiceClient with DefaultAzureCredential. Use date-stamped blob name (yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json) and a conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) to prevent duplicate writes on same-day retrigger. Log blob_write_succeeded with blob URI.
inputs: INF-01 output (storage account name, container name)
outputs: src/blob_writer.py — conditional blob writer with Managed Identity, idempotency, structured logging
acceptance_criteria: BlobServiceClient instantiated with DefaultAzureCredential (no connection strings)
Blob name format: {run_date_utc}.json (UTC date, not local)
upload_blob called with overwrite=False and If-None-Match: * header to enforce conditional PUT
ResourceExistsError caught and handled gracefully (idempotent — log warning, do not raise)
blob_write_succeeded log event includes full blob URI
Storage account name read from environment variable (not hardcoded)
risks: If the function runs after midnight UTC on same calendar day due to retry, conditional PUT prevents a double-write — verify this is tested

tasks.title: Implement GetAndStoreYankeesRoster Timer Trigger function
tasks.description: Implement src/function_app.py with the GetAndStoreYankeesRoster Timer Trigger function using the Azure Functions v2 programming model. Configure schedule '0 0 2 * * *', useMonitor=True, and explicit UTC timezone. Orchestrate TRAPI call → validation → blob write → failure path. Set functionTimeout: 120 and maxConcurrentCalls: 1 in host.json.
outputs: src/function_app.py — Timer Trigger function
host.json — updated with functionTimeout:120, maxConcurrentCalls:1
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger schedule: '0 0 2 * * *' (6-part CRON for Azure Functions)
useMonitor=True set on trigger decorator
function_started and function_completed log events emitted
On validation success: blob written, player_count_returned custom metric emitted
On validation failure: failed response written to failed/ prefix, alert triggered
host.json functionTimeout: '00:02:00', extensions.queues.maxPollingInterval not required (Timer Trigger)
maxConcurrentCalls not applicable to Timer Trigger — singleton behavior enforced by useMonitor
risks: functionTimeout of 120s on Consumption Plan may not be extendable beyond 10 minutes — 120s is well within limit
Cold start on Consumption Plan adds 2–5s to first invocation

tasks.title: Implement failed-response handler (write to failed/ prefix)
tasks.description: Implement src/failure_handler.py to write raw TRAPI response JSON to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json when schema validation fails or player count is out of range. Emit a structured log event and raise a custom exception to trigger Application Insights failure alert.
outputs: src/failure_handler.py — failure blob writer + structured error logging
acceptance_criteria: Failed blob written to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json
Raw response written as-is (no mutation) for debugging
Structured log event includes validation_error, player_count_received, run_date_utc
Exception raised after writing failed blob (so Application Insights captures execution failure)
Does not write to primary yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json path
risks: If storage write also fails in the failure path, the exception chain must be handled to avoid masking the original validation error

tasks.title: Implement structured logging and custom metric emission
tasks.description: Integrate OpenTelemetry SDK into the function to emit structured log events (function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed) with defined fields. Emit the player_count_returned custom metric via Application Insights on each successful run. Enable adaptive sampling in host.json.
outputs: src/observability.py — logger helpers and custom metric emitter
requirements-prod.txt — updated with opentelemetry-sdk deps if needed
acceptance_criteria: All five key log events emitted with documented fields (model_version, prompt_hash, token_count, latency_ms, player_count, blob_uri)
player_count_returned custom metric emitted via trackMetric or OpenTelemetry counter
Logs appear in Application Insights under customEvents or traces table
Adaptive sampling configured in host.json (logging.applicationInsights.samplingSettings)
No PII or sensitive data logged
risks: OpenTelemetry + Azure Functions v2 integration requires correct SDK version pinning — validate against azure-functions SDK compatibility matrix

tasks.title: Configure Key Vault references in Function App settings
tasks.description: Configure all sensitive Function App settings as Azure Key Vault references (@Microsoft.KeyVault(SecretUri=...)) in the Bicep Function App module. Ensure TRAPI-related settings (if any credentials are needed per DES-01) are stored as Key Vault secrets and referenced — not inlined. Validate Key Vault references resolve correctly after deployment.
inputs: DES-01 output (auth mechanism — credential needed or not)
outputs: infra/modules/functionapp.bicep — updated with @Microsoft.KeyVault() references for sensitive settings
acceptance_criteria: No plaintext secrets in app settings (connection strings, API keys, tokens)
All Key Vault references use SecretUri format with specific version pinned for production
Function App Managed Identity has Key Vault Secrets User role (from INF-05)
Key Vault reference resolution verified via Azure Portal or az functionapp config appsettings list
local.settings.json.example updated to reflect required env var names without values
risks: Key Vault reference resolution failures silently set the app setting to empty string — add a startup validation check

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights alert rules (failure, duration, data quality)
tasks.description: Author infra/modules/alerts.bicep to provision three Application Insights alert rules: (1) execution failure count > 0 in 1-hour window with email action; (2) execution duration > 90 seconds; (3) custom metric player_count_returned < 24 or > 40. Wire to an Action Group notifying rciapala via email.
outputs: infra/modules/alerts.bicep — three alert rules + email Action Group
acceptance_criteria: Execution failure alert: scheduled query rule on exceptions table, threshold > 0, window 1h, email notification
Duration alert: metric alert on Function App duration metric > 90000ms
Data quality alert: metric alert on player_count_returned custom metric with dynamic threshold or static < 24 or > 40
Action Group created with rciapala email as receiver
All alerts provisioned in staging before production
Bicep lints cleanly
risks: Custom metric player_count_returned alert requires the metric to be emitted at least once before the alert rule can be validated — test in staging after first successful run

tasks.title: Implement unit tests for TRAPI client, validator, and blob writer
tasks.description: Implement tests/test_trapi_client.py, tests/test_validator.py, and tests/test_blob_writer.py using pytest and unittest.mock. Cover: prompt loading, auth token acquisition mock, retry logic, schema validation (valid/invalid/boundary player counts), conditional PUT behavior, and failure path routing.
outputs: tests/test_trapi_client.py
tests/test_validator.py
tests/test_blob_writer.py
tests/test_failure_handler.py
acceptance_criteria: All tests pass: python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing
Coverage >= 80% on src/trapi_client.py, src/validator.py, src/blob_writer.py
Retry logic tested: mock 2x 500 responses followed by 200 — assert 3 total attempts
Validator tests cover: valid 24-player response, valid 28-player response, 23-player (fail), 29-player (fail), missing field (fail)
Conditional PUT test: assert ResourceExistsError is caught and does not propagate
Failure handler test: assert failed blob path is yankees-roster/failed/{date}.json, not primary path
risks: Mocking DefaultAzureCredential and BlobServiceClient requires careful patch targeting — use unittest.mock.patch at the module import level

tasks.title: Implement integration test for known player assertions
tasks.description: Implement tests/test_integration.py with a live or recorded integration test that verifies the blob output in the yankees-roster container contains Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson. Use pytest markers to gate this test behind an 'integration' mark so it does not run in CI by default.
outputs: tests/test_integration.py — known player assertion integration test
acceptance_criteria: Test reads the most recent blob from yankees-roster container using Managed Identity
Asserts Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, and Rickey Henderson are present in players array
Asserts player count is between 24 and 28
Test marked with @pytest.mark.integration and excluded from CI default run
pytest.ini updated to register 'integration' marker
risks: Live integration test requires deployed Azure resources and valid Managed Identity — may be flaky in CI if credentials are unavailable

tasks.title: GitHub Actions: CI workflow (unit tests on push to main)
tasks.description: Implement .github/workflows/ci.yml triggered on push to main, disabled by default (workflow_dispatch as secondary trigger or enable flag). Installs requirements.txt, runs pytest unit tests (excluding integration marker), and reports coverage. No Azure credentials required for unit tests.
outputs: .github/workflows/ci.yml — CI workflow, disabled by default
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggered on push to main AND workflow_dispatch
Workflow disabled by default (using workflow enable/disable or conditional step)
Installs dependencies via pip install -r requirements.txt
Runs pytest -m 'not integration' with coverage report
Workflow fails if any unit test fails
No Azure credentials or secrets in workflow
risks: 'Disabled by default' requires a manual enable step after repo setup — document in README

tasks.title: GitHub Actions: CD Infra workflow (provision Azure resources)
tasks.description: Implement .github/workflows/cd-infra.yml triggered manually with an environment parameter. Authenticates via OIDC (from REL-01), runs az deployment group create with main.bicep, and runs a post-deploy validation test (e.g., verify storage account and Function App exist, RBAC assignments are correct).
outputs: .github/workflows/cd-infra.yml — manual infra provisioning workflow with validation step
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers via workflow_dispatch with environment input (dev/staging/prod)
Uses azure/login with OIDC (client-id, tenant-id, subscription-id from repo variables — no secrets)
Runs az deployment group create --template-file infra/main.bicep
Post-deploy validation: asserts storage account, function app, key vault, app insights exist
Workflow fails on Bicep deployment error
No hardcoded resource names — all parameterized via env inputs or Bicep parameters file
risks: Bicep what-if diff not run before apply — consider adding --what-if step for safety on production runs

tasks.title: GitHub Actions: CD App workflow (deploy function, run smoke test)
tasks.description: Implement .github/workflows/cd-app.yml triggered manually with an environment parameter. Authenticates via OIDC, deploys the function app using func azure functionapp publish or az functionapp deployment, and runs a smoke test that verifies the function app is running and the timer trigger is registered.
outputs: .github/workflows/cd-app.yml — manual app deployment workflow with smoke test
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers via workflow_dispatch with environment input
Deploys to staging slot by default; environment param controls target
OIDC authentication only — no client secrets
Smoke test verifies Function App is running (az functionapp show --query state) and GetAndStoreYankeesRoster trigger is listed
Deployment fails workflow if func deploy exits non-zero
risks: func CLI version must be pinned in workflow to avoid breaking changes from newer CLI releases

tasks.title: GitHub Actions: CD Promote workflow (stage-to-prod slot swap)
tasks.description: Implement .github/workflows/cd-promote.yml triggered manually with source (default: staging) and target (default: prod) environment parameters. Performs an Azure deployment slot swap or resource-group-level blue/green promotion. Validates the production function app is healthy post-swap.
outputs: .github/workflows/cd-promote.yml — manual promote workflow with post-swap health check
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers via workflow_dispatch with source_env and target_env inputs (defaults: staging, prod)
Performs az functionapp deployment slot swap or equivalent promotion
Post-swap health check: verify production Function App state = Running
OIDC authentication only
Workflow emits a deployment summary with source version, target, and timestamp
risks: Consumption Plan slot swap availability must be confirmed (open question) — fallback to blue/green resource group swap if slots unsupported

tasks.title: End-to-end staging validation (nightly timer and blob output)
tasks.description: Deploy to staging environment and validate the complete end-to-end flow: timer fires at 02:00 UTC (or manually triggered), TRAPI is called, response validated, blob written to staging storage account. Run integration tests against staging blob. Verify all three Application Insights alerts are active.
outputs: results/staging-e2e-validation.md — test run results with blob URI, player count, alert verification
acceptance_criteria: Function executes successfully in staging (exit code 0, no exceptions in App Insights)
Blob yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json exists in staging storage with 24–28 players
Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson present in blob output
Conditional PUT: second manual trigger on same day does not overwrite blob (ResourceExistsError logged, not raised)
All three Application Insights alerts visible in Azure Portal for staging workspace
player_count_returned custom metric appears in App Insights metrics explorer
risks: TRAPI rate limits may cause staging tests to fail if run frequently — coordinate with TRAPI team on staging quota

tasks.title: Production deployment via CD Promote slot swap
tasks.description: Execute the CD Promote workflow to swap staging to production. Verify production Function App is healthy, Application Insights alerts are active in production workspace, and the first production blob write is confirmed. Tag the release commit.
outputs: Git release tag (e.g., v1.0.0)
results/production-deployment-confirmation.md — post-deploy verification checklist
acceptance_criteria: CD Promote workflow completes without errors
Production Function App state: Running
First production blob write confirmed (next 02:00 UTC run or manual trigger)
All three alert rules active in production Application Insights workspace
Git release tag created and push to main confirmed
Rollback plan documented (re-run CD Promote in reverse if needed)
risks: First production run may encounter TRAPI latency spike on cold start — monitor App Insights for duration > 90s alert in first 24h

tasks.title: Write README with local dev, TRAPI auth, blob naming, and CI/CD instructions
tasks.description: Write a comprehensive README.md covering: local development setup (virtualenv, local.settings.json, func start), TRAPI authentication instructions (Managed Identity scope, local az login fallback), blob naming convention, CI/CD workflow usage (how to run each workflow, required GitHub variables), and OIDC federated identity setup steps.
outputs: README.md — complete developer and operator guide
acceptance_criteria: Local dev setup section: virtualenv creation, pip install -r requirements.txt, local.settings.json config, func start command
TRAPI auth section: Managed Identity scope (api://trapi/.default), az login for local dev, token acquisition steps
Blob naming convention documented: yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json and failed/ prefix
CI/CD section: step-by-step for each of the 5 workflows including required GitHub variables/secrets
OIDC federated identity setup: step-by-step guide to run CD Setup and configure GitHub repo variables
README reviewed and approved by rciapala
risks: OIDC setup instructions may become stale if Azure AD or GitHub Actions OIDC flow changes — include links to official docs

tasks.title: Production smoke test and monitoring validation
tasks.description: After production deployment, run a manual trigger of the function (or wait for first nightly execution), verify the production blob is written correctly, confirm all Application Insights alerts are firing correctly with a test exception, and sign off all success criteria in spec.md.
outputs: results/production-smoke-test.md — signed-off success criteria checklist
acceptance_criteria: Production blob yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json exists with 24–28 players including Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson
Function execution duration < 60 seconds logged in App Insights
Execution failure alert fires on injected test failure and email received by rciapala
player_count_returned metric visible in production App Insights metrics explorer
